rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts; use only visible features or declared helpers.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers are resolvable and types match between declarations and expressions.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE annexes to avoid resolution errors.
Validate that property associations and data types in AGREE code align with the AADL model's package and type system.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE clauses must use valid syntax: assume, guarantee, eq, const, and assign with correct type annotations.
AGREE scalar types are int, real, and bool; avoid referencing AADL package-qualified types not present in the model.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or property references that are not declared in the AADL model.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair strategies
